docs.title: Human-Centered Programming by Demonstration for Collaborative Industrial Robotics
docs.text: The HUBOT Chair is an innovative and interdisciplinary research project funded under the MIAI Cluster IA until June 2030. It aims to bridge fundamental AI research and industrial application by shifting how collaborative robots are programmed in the era of Industry 4.0.

docs.title: Chair Leadership & Contacts
docs.text:
Damien Pellier — Chair Holder
  • Position: Professor of Computer Science, Université Grenoble Alpes
  • Lab / Team: LIG (UMR 5217) — Head of Marvin Team
  • Office: Room 365, IMAG Building, Grenoble
  • Contact: +33 4 57 42 15 39 | damien.pellier@imag.fr

  • Lab / Team: Institut Pascal (UMR 6602 - UCA/CNRS) — CaVITI Team

  • Contact: +33 4 73 17 71 85 | flavien.paccot@uca.fr

docs.title: 🧠 Project Overview & Objectives
docs.text:
In the context of Industry 4.0, reprogramming industrial robots remains a costly and complex task requiring expert intervention. Rigid, traditional programming approaches lack the agility needed for modern manufacturing environments characterized by high product variability.

The HUBOT Chair proposes an innovative framework based on Programming by Demonstration (PbD), enabling non-expert operators to teach collaborative robots through intuitive kinesthetic, haptic, or visual interactions.

docs.title: Key Research Pillars:
docs.text:
• Neuro-Symbolic Learning: Designing advanced learning algorithms capable of extracting and generalizing trajectories from a limited number of human demonstrations.
  • Semantic Task Models: Incorporating models that capture high-level task intentions, formal logic, and constraints (PDDL/HTN).
  • Co-Manipulation Models: Developing a novel co-manipulation framework that facilitates natural human-robot collaboration without requiring users to adapt to the robot.
  • Cognitive Ergonomics & Health: Investigating acceptability, transparency, and comprehension factors, leveraging medical and clinical insights for human operator well-being in industrial tasks.

The ultimate goal of the HUBOT Chair is to produce a human-centered, semantically aware robotic demonstrator, marking a significant step toward intelligent, adaptive, and trustworthy robotics for the factories of the future.

docs.title: 🤝 Partners & Academic Network
docs.text:
The HUBOT Chair thrives on a strong ecosystem combining cutting-edge academic labs, medical institutions, and major industrial leaders:

  • Academic & Institutional Partners:
  • LIG - Laboratoire d'Informatique de Grenoble (Marvin Team, Université Grenoble Alpes)
  • Institut Pascal (CaVITI Team, UMR 6602 - UCA/CNRS)
  • Industrial Leaders:
  • Stäubli
  • Kassow Robots

docs.title: 📬 Join the Project / Contact
docs.text: We are actively looking for talent, collaboration, and industrial use cases. If you are interested in joining the HUBOT project or have any inquiries regarding our automated planning and robotics framework, please feel free to reach out to either co-runner.

docs.title: Open PhD Positions — MIAI Hubot Chair
docs.text: The MIAI Hubot Chair, in collaboration with the Laboratoire d'informatique de Grenoble (LIG) (Université Grenoble Alpes) and the Institut Pascal (Université Clermont Auvergne / CNRS), is looking for talented and motivated candidates to fill two fully funded PhD positions starting in September 2026. Both positions are secured for a duration of 36 months by the Chair.

docs.title: 1. PhD Position: Neuro-Symbolic Learning of Planning Domains
docs.text:
• Framework: Funded by the MIAI Hubot Chair
  • Topic: Applied to Programming by Demonstration for Collaborative Robotics
  • Hosting Labs: LIG (Marvin team) & Institut Pascal
  • Supervisor: Damien Pellier
  • Key Skills: Python, PyTorch / TensorFlow, PDDL, Formal Logic
  • 👉 Read the detailed job description (PDF)

docs.title: 2. PhD Position: Robust HTN Planning Under Uncertainty
docs.text:
• Framework: Funded by the MIAI Hubot Chair
  • Topic: Extension of HTN formalisms based on an interval representation of uncertainties
  • Hosting Labs: LIG (Marvin team) & Institut Pascal
  • Supervisor: Damien Pellier
  • Key Skills: Rust / C++, Automated Planning, Constraint Programming or Optimization
  • 👉 Read the detailed job description (PDF)

docs.title: Application Procedures
docs.text:
Interested candidates must send an application package including a CV, a cover letter, Master's transcripts (M1/M2), and contact details for at least one academic reference to the PhD supervisor**.

  • Application Deadline: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the positions are filled.
